# Divider

The divider component creates horizontal rules with customizable thickness, width, color, and positioning. It uses CSS custom properties for flexible theming and can be styled to match various design contexts.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the divider's properties in real-time.

<Canvas of={DividerStories.Interactive} />
<Controls of={DividerStories.Interactive} />

## Properties

### Optional Properties

{twigPropsTable(componentProps, 'optional')}

## CSS Custom Properties

The divider can be styled using CSS custom properties:

- `--thickness-divider`: Controls line thickness (uses design tokens like `var(--size-thickness-hairline)`, `var(--size-thickness-1)`, etc.)
- `--color-divider`: Controls line color (uses color tokens like `var(--color-gray-500)`, `var(--color-blue-yale)`)
- `--width-theme-divider`: Controls divider width (uses width tokens)

## Variations

### Thickness Options

The divider supports multiple thickness values through design tokens:

- Hairline (thinnest)
- 1px
- 2px
- 4px
- 6px
- 8px (thickest)

### Width Options

Four width presets are available:

- 25% width
- 50% width
- 75% width
- 100% width (full width)

### Position Options

- **Left**: Aligns divider to the left edge
- **Center**: Centers the divider horizontally

### Color Variations

The divider color can be customized to match themed backgrounds:

- Gray (default)
- Blue Yale
- Brown-gray
- Any design system color token

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Atom
- **Template**: `yds-divider.twig`
- **Styles**: `cl-dividers.scss`

### Accessibility

- Uses semantic `<hr>` element for proper semantic meaning
- Provides visual separation that can be detected by screen readers
- Color is not the only means of conveying separation

### Behavior

- **Static Element**: No interactive behavior
- **Responsive**: Maintains proportions across breakpoints
- **Themeable**: Adapts to section theme contexts

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Use dividers to create visual separation between content sections
- Match divider color to the current theme for visual harmony
- Use appropriate thickness for the visual weight needed
- Center position works well for decorative dividers
- Left position works well for content-aligned dividers

### Things to Avoid

- Don't overuse dividers - whitespace can provide separation
- Avoid using dividers as the only means of visual hierarchy
- Don't use very thick dividers in dense content areas
- Avoid color choices that don't provide sufficient contrast

## Implementation Notes

### Theming

The divider integrates with the design system's theming through CSS custom properties. Set the color using the `--color-divider` custom property with design token values:

```css
--color-divider: var(--color-gray-500);
```

### Thickness Control

Set thickness using the `--thickness-divider` custom property:

```css
--thickness-divider: var(--size-thickness-2);
```

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all divider variations and theme combinations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/atoms-divider-visreg).
